entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A drought tolerant crop model used in the study to identify genes for drought tolerance

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A predominant constraint on crop yield simulated in the study using polyethylene glycol

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the crop, which is negatively impacted by drought

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain growth and yield under drought stress

entities.name: ALLELES
entities.description: Novel genetic variants in sorghum landraces that contribute to local adaptation

entities.name: LOCAL ADAPTATION
entities.description: The process by which landraces adapt to specific local environmental conditions

entities.name: SHOOT GROWTH
entities.description: The growth of the above-ground part of the plant, which was reduced by PEG

entities.name: ROOT GROWTH
entities.description: The growth of the below-ground part of the plant, evaluated under simulated drought

entities.name: POLYETHYLENE GLYCOL
entities.description: A chemical used to simulate drought conditions at 10% and 20% concentrations

entities.name: SEEDLING TOLERANCE COEFFICIENT
entities.description: A metric used to measure and quantify drought tolerance

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: The evaluation of observable characteristics such as root number and length

entities.name: ROOT NUMBER
entities.description: The quantity of roots produced by the plant under drought stress

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH
entities.description: The length of the roots produced by the plant under drought stress

entities.name: GENOMEWIDE ASSOCIATION STUDY
entities.description: A method used to identify genomic loci associated with growth and STC traits

entities.name: LOCI
entities.description: Specific genomic positions identified by GWAS associated with drought tolerance traits

entities.name: SHOOT WEIGHT
entities.description: A specific trait measured as part of the Seedling Tolerance Coefficient

entities.name: QTLS
entities.description: Quantitative Trait Loci previously mapped for drought-related traits

entities.name: GREEN LEAF AREA
entities.description: A trait mapped to drought-related QTLs

entities.name: TOTAL NUMBER OF GREEN LEAVES
entities.description: A trait mapped to drought-related QTLs

entities.name: CHLOROPHYLL CONTENT
entities.description: A trait mapped to drought-related QTLs

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENES
entities.description: Genes identified for specific loci that may contribute to drought tolerance

entities.name: GENEATLAS V2
entities.description: A tool used to analyze the expression of candidate genes in roots

entities.name: CHLOROPHYLL FLUORESCENCE
entities.description: A trait associated with a specific QTL and candidate gene

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A crop in which a candidate gene was shown to increase chlorophyll fluorescence

entities.name: IS 30533
entities.description: A sorghum accession identified as the most drought tolerant

entities.name: IS 32439
entities.description: A sorghum accession identified as the most drought sensitive

relationships.description: Sorghum is used as a model for studying drought tolerance
Drought acts as a constraint on crop yield
Sorghum landraces possess novel alleles
Alleles contribute to local adaptation
Shoot growth was evaluated in sorghum
Root growth was evaluated in sorghum
Polyethylene glycol is used to simulate drought conditions
Polyethylene glycol reduced shoot growth
Seedling Tolerance Coefficient is used to measure drought tolerance
Phenotypic analysis measured root number
Phenotypic analysis measured root length
GWAS identified 22 loci
Loci were mapped to STC traits
Loci were mapped to STC of shoot weight
Loci were colocated with previously mapped QTLs
QTLs were mapped to green leaf area
QTLs were mapped to total number of green leaves
QTLs were mapped to chlorophyll content
Candidate genes were identified for specific loci
GeneAtlas v2 was used to analyze expression of candidate genes
A candidate gene increases chlorophyll fluorescence
Chlorophyll fluorescence was studied in maize
IS 30533 is the most tolerant accession
IS 32439 is the most sensitive accession
